SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.key

Class DefaultKeyGenerator

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.key.DefaultKeyGenerator
All Implemented Interfaces:
KeyGenerator
public class DefaultKeyGenerator extends Object implements KeyGenerator
Default KeyGenerator that derives a cache key from method arguments.

Key construction rules per argument:

  • No args (null or empty list) → "NO-ARG"
  • Primitive, Number, String, or Boolean → String.valueOf(Object)
  • Collection → elements converted recursively, joined by ","
  • Array → elements converted recursively, joined by ","
  • Any type that overrides Object.toString() (records, enums, value objects) → its toString() value, which is deterministic and stable across JVM restarts
  • Any other type (identity toString()) → "ClassName@hashCode" (hex); a warning is logged because the identity hash is unstable across JVM restarts and will cause failover lookups to miss with a persistent store. Override toString/equals/ hashCode or configure a custom KeyGenerator

Multiple arguments are joined with ":".

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:

    key(Failover failover, List<Object> args)
    Generates a cache key by converting each argument to its string representation and joining all arguments with ":".

  • Constructor Details

      public DefaultKeyGenerator()

  • Method Details

    • key

      public String key(Failover failover, List<Object> args)
      Generates a cache key by converting each argument to its string representation and joining all arguments with ":".

      Returns "NO-ARG" when args is null or empty. See the class-level documentation for per-argument type rules.

      Specified by:
      key in interface KeyGenerator
      Parameters:
      failover - annotation metadata; used in warning logs for unrecognised argument types
      args - resolved method arguments; may be null or empty
      Returns:
      non-null key string
